title: The Utah Blanket Wars
byline: By Pentimento, staff writer, from Kyle Killen's field notes and original reporting
thesis: Two luxury-blanket brands fighting each other on Utah freeway billboards are a live field study in tribal cognition — the same failure modes the machine pieces dissect, observed in the wild at seventy miles an hour.
description: The human-cognition mirror to the type specimen. Six movements from the freeway to the freeway: costly signaling with product removed as a variable, the Berger contamination experiment, Inglehart's actuarial table, the crocodile's two arcs — and the machine arriving for the signal this publication is made of. The central field observation — that one brand pivoted its imagery in answer to the other — rests on the founder's own eyewitness record and could not be independently sourced; the piece prints that uncertainty openly, as an assignment wearing the costume of a fact. Every out-of-brief figure was verified live at revision with named sources; the sourcing pass corrected one price upward.
